name: Expense Auto-Categorization Engine
summary: Automated expense categorization and approval/denial system to replace manual email-based expense review for client relocations.
description:
## Problem
Our current expense review process is entirely manual and email-based. AMs receive expense receipts from relocated employees, manually categorize them (equipment, furniture, internet, coworking, etc.), check them against per-client policy limits, and approve/deny via email. This is slow (~3 day turnaround), error-prone (we've had multiple mis-categorizations this quarter), and doesn't scale as we onboard more clients.

## Proposed Solution
Build an auto-categorization engine that:
1. Ingests submitted expense receipts
2. Auto-categorizes by expense type
3. Validates against per-client policy rules (limits, excluded categories)
4. Auto-approves or flags for manual review based on confidence + policy match

## Success Criteria
- Reduce expense review turnaround from ~3 days to <4 hours
- 95%+ categorization accuracy on standard expense types
- Per-client configurable policy rules
- Admin dashboard for manual override and audit trail

name: DOT Compliance Audit — Q1/Q2 2026 Hazmat Shipments
summary: Audit and compile complete DOT hazardous materials documentation for all hazmat-classified shipments coordinated by MoveOps in Q1-Q2 2026, in response to PHMSA formal compliance inquiry Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187.
description: Triggered by a formal DOT compliance inquiry from PHMSA (Linda Castellano, Compliance Investigations Branch). PHMSA inspected Swift Relocations' carrier records and identified MoveOps as shipper of record on multiple hazmat-classified shipments. DOT is requesting complete documentation packages — shipping papers, placarding certificates, carrier certifications, emergency response documentation, and training records — for all hazmat shipments in the Jan 1 - Jun 30, 2026 window. Response deadline: 30 calendar days from April 21, 2026 (extension request submitted). Financial exposure: up to $59,017 per violation per day under 49 CFR 107.329. Four moves identified in scope. Three have complete documentation; one (Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter) has a missing placarding certificate requiring urgent remediation.
